rulings.Case Title: PENSIRI COHN ET AL VS. DANIEL O'NEILL ET AL (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate The Trial Or In The Alt. Regulate The Order Of Proof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 10. DEFENDANT DANIEL O'NEILL's Motion To Bifurcate The Trial Or In The Alt. Regulate The Order Of Proof deferred to the trial judge, who is best equipped to manage proceedings in her or his court; it is thus DENIED without prejudice. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PENSIRI COHN ET AL VS. DANIEL O'NEILL ET AL (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Motion For Leave To File Supplemental/Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 10. Defendants' Motion For Leave To File Supplemental/Amended Answer GRANTED. Trial courts are to liberally permit amendment of pleadings; an answer may be amended to raise a settlement as an affirmative defense. Nicholson, 233 CA3d at 1681. Supplemental answer to be filed within five court days. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM H BRADLEY VS. DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT RSM&A FORECLOSURE SERVICES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication DENIED. The motion is premised on assertions that a 2007 deed of trust encumbered both 637 Peralta and 639 Peralta. See, e.g., UMF 25. However, in denying three other defendants' motions for summary adjudication, the Court already found triable issues of material fact as to, inter alia, whether a legal description was attached to the deed of trust only after plaintiff signed it. See, e.g., 5/10/16 Order p. 5 (that order is incorporated here by reference). This would void the deed of trust as to 639 Peralta. When "a deed is altered or changed by someone other than the grantor before it is delivered or recorded, and the alteration is without the grantor's knowledge or consent, the deed is void and no title vests in the grantee or subsequent purchasers, even bona fide purchasers for value." 3 Miller & Starr, Cal. Real Estate (3d ed. 2011) sec. 8:53; Montgomery, 85 CA 2d at 563; Lin, 232 CA4th at 714-15. Further, triable issues of material fact exist as to whether plaintiff's claims are time-barred, mistakes were unilateral or mutual and RSM&S breached its statutory duties and instructions. See, e.g., UMF 2-3, 8, 11, 14, 18, 23, 25, 27-29, 39, 43, 45-49, 51, 53-54, 57; AMF 1-23, 25-58. The RSM&A acts alleged by plaintiff are not "privileged communications" under Civil Code sec. 2924(d). = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LEW ROTHGEB VS. GREG DE FOREST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT's LEW ROTHGEB and GREG BRYAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The plain meaning of San Francisco Rent Ordinance §37.9A(e)(3) is that one-half of the relocation payments "shall be paid at the time of service of the Notice of Termination of Tenancy." Nothing about this language is conditional; the ordinance does not say, e.g., that the amount is merely to be "deposited" or "lodged." It is logical that a tenant may well begin the expense of seeking a new place to live when she receives such a notice. Thus, as a matter of law, cross-complainants cannot make out a cause of action for conversion, and this legal issue is susceptible to demurrer. See, e.g., Moore, 51 Cal.3d 120. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

rulings.Case Title: SHENG-WEI LO ET AL VS. ROSALIA ELIAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 8. DEFENDANT ROSALIA ELIAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. The Declarant, Ms. Elias, is not a signatory to the purported lease, attached to her declaration. Additionally, the Estoppel Certificate did not contain any information regarding "prepaid rent." = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: YINMING INVESTMENT CO & WINGTAI CO. VS. MAI KIM TRAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 17, 2016, line 9. DEFENDANT MAI TRAN, LAN TRAN, HAI NGO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED, subject to opposition. Moving party shifted its burden and presented sufficient evidence re: creation of tenancy/sub tenancy in 1997 or 2006. = (501/RBU)
